ledger_scope: Initial source evidence ledger for Big Five Result Page V2 backend content-asset agent design and future selector asset QA.
negative_guarantees: No selector assets are generated by this ledger.
No runtime wrapper, pilot gate, production import gate, sitemap, llms, or search queue behavior is changed.

No BFI-2 item text, proprietary report copy, competitor copy, private score payload, raw score, vector, percentile, attempt id, editor notes, QA notes, or internal metadata is copied into public payloads.

sources.title: International Personality Item Pool official site
permitted_use: Use as public-domain Big Five/IPIP measurement context.
Use to support statements that IPIP items and scales are open source context for personality measurement.
Use as citation context for future source-ledger rows that need public-domain measurement provenance.
limitations: Public-domain source status does not make generated FermatMind result-page copy automatically safe, validated, or production-ready.
IPIP context does not authorize deterministic type labels, diagnosis, hiring-screening, success prediction, or ability claims.
Future generated assets still require selector contract, safety, claim, route, and rendered preview QA.
disallowed_use: Do not treat IPIP as proof of private user percentile, norm eligibility, or production score interpretation.
Do not expose raw item responses, raw scores, domain vectors, or facet vectors in public/share payloads.

copy_policy.notes: IPIP source material is public domain, but FermatMind public payloads must still be locally authored and validated.

claim_trace_requirements: Claims must cite this source_id and a method-boundary limitation.
Claims must not imply official FermatMind norm eligibility or percentile availability.

evidence_refs.summary: The official IPIP site describes IPIP as a pool of items and scales and states that its items and scales are public domain.
limitations: This supports source-use permissions, not FermatMind runtime readiness.

sources.title: The Big Five Inventory-2 (BFI-2), Colby Personality Lab
permitted_use: Use as structure reference for Big Five domain and facet framing.
Use as evidence that a mainstream BFI-2 framing includes five domains and 15 more-specific facets.
Use to keep FermatMind facet claims weaker than independent measurement unless local support fields prove otherwise.
limitations: BFI-2 items are copyrighted and permission is limited by the source owner.
This ledger treats BFI-2 as structure reference only, not a copy source.
BFI-2 structure does not authorize official 32-type claims or deterministic labels.
disallowed_use: Do not copy BFI-2 items, scoring text, report copy, or body prose.
Do not represent FermatMind assets as official BFI-2 reports.
Do not use BFI-2 as support for hiring, diagnosis, therapy, or success prediction claims.

copy_policy.notes: Use only high-level structure and method framing; no item text or report prose may be copied.

claim_trace_requirements: Facet-related claims must include a limitation that facets are interpretation supports unless local measurement support is present.
Any generated facet asset must pass `BigFiveResultPageV2SelectorAssetValidator` independent-measurement checks.

evidence_refs.summary: The Colby BFI-2 page describes BFI-2 as measuring five Big Five domains and 15 more-specific facet traits.
limitations: Structure support only; no copy permission for item/report text.

sources.title: University of Oregon: Measuring the Big Five Personality Domains
permitted_use: Use as method framing that Big Five domains are broad trait dimensions.
Use as support for describing Big Five as descriptive and dimensional rather than fixed personality types.
Use to keep public payloads bounded to partial personality description and person-perception framing.
limitations: The page is a public educational method source, not a FermatMind validation report.
It does not authorize private score disclosure, percentile claims, diagnosis, hiring, or treatment claims.
disallowed_use: Do not use this source to claim Big Five explains the whole person.
Do not turn broad trait dimensions into official type labels or deterministic outcomes.
Do not use it as a copy source for FermatMind result-page body prose.

copy_policy.notes: Use for high-level method boundary and dimensional framing only.

claim_trace_requirements: Claims must preserve descriptive, dimensional, and partial-description boundaries.
Claims must not imply comprehensive personality diagnosis or predictive certainty.

evidence_refs.summary: The University of Oregon page frames Big Five as broad trait dimensions and notes that even comprehensive trait profiles are partial descriptions.
limitations: Supports method boundary, not product validation or production readiness.

sources.title: Alheimsins bigfive-web GitHub repository
permitted_use: Use as open-source implementation reference for Big Five web application packaging and IPIP-based test flow context.
Use to compare non-copy architecture patterns such as separation of test flow and result rendering.
limitations: Open-source project structure is not FermatMind source authority for result copy.
Repository popularity or implementation existence does not validate FermatMind private result assets.
disallowed_use: Do not copy product-facing result-page prose, report copy, UI copy, or scoring presentation into FermatMind assets.
Do not import repository data or respondent-like samples into FermatMind content assets.

copy_policy.notes: Use only as architecture/reference context; not as a content source.

claim_trace_requirements: Any reference must state implementation-reference-only status and must not support reader-facing claims.

evidence_refs.summary: The repository describes a Big Five web app based on IPIP-NEO-PI and IPIP-sourced tests/evaluation.
limitations: Implementation reference only; not a copy source or psychometric validation source for FermatMind.

sources.title: FermatMind Big Five 新版结果页｜正式上线 V2.0
permitted_use: Use as module master for Big Five Result Page V2 module names, reading modes, interaction modules, and source planning.
Use to trace result-page asset scope back to internal V2 module authority.
limitations: Internal authority does not bypass selector validation, safety QA, route QA, rendered preview, or production gates.
The source authority package is governance-only and not runtime content.
disallowed_use: Do not wire the V2.0 document directly into runtime or frontend fallback.
Do not generalize O59 or draft body copy to all users without selector and route QA.
Do not use it to mark assets pilot-ready or production-ready.

copy_policy.notes: Internal source authority may guide module structure and claim scope; body copy requires a separate approved asset batch.

claim_trace_requirements: Every derived module claim must cite the source authority path and preserve runtime_use/not_runtime boundary.

evidence_refs.summary: The source authority map identifies the V2.0 formal doc as module_master for module structure, V2 reading system, and module 00-10 naming baseline.
limitations: Governance-only; no runtime wiring or selector QA pass implied.

sources.title: FermatMind｜Big Five 完整结果页全文（最终成稿）
permitted_use: Use as canonical O59 longform body source trace for the O59/C32/E20/A55/N68 staging profile.
Use to map O59 core body sections to V2 modules and the eight-section result skeleton.
limitations: This source is canonical O59 staging content only.
It is not generalized to all 3125 combinations or all users.
It remains staging-only and production-disallowed until rendered preview and downstream gates pass.
disallowed_use: Do not use O59 longform body as universal Big Five result copy.
Do not use as source for non-O59 canonical profiles without a separate source ledger and asset batch.
Do not expose private score tuple or internal combination key in share-safe payloads.

copy_policy.notes: Internal longform can trace O59 staging copy only inside approved asset batches; this ledger does not create copy.

claim_trace_requirements: Claims must include O59-only limitation and source_trace section reference.
Claims must not be used for share-safe public payloads unless transformed by share_safety_registry.

evidence_refs.summary: The O59 source trace identifies the longform final draft as narrative_master/canonical_body_master for O59 canonical 8-section core body.
limitations: O59 staging-only body source; no generalization or runtime readiness implied.

sources.title: Existing Big Five Result Page V2 asset packs and master catalog
permitted_use: Use as current backend inventory for package count, readiness flags, and existing staged asset surfaces.
Use to verify that existing assets remain not-runtime or staging-only before future repair/generation work.
Use as input to gap audit and validator harness tasks.
limitations: Master catalog reports asset-review readiness only, not pilot/runtime/production readiness.
Existing packs include staging candidates and governance artifacts; they are not a production asset source.
disallowed_use: Do not treat existing package presence as proof that selector coverage, safety QA, editorial QA, route QA, or rendered preview QA passed.
Do not import into CMS or wire runtime based only on catalog presence.
Do not use staging selector candidates as final public payloads without validator and safety reports.

copy_policy.notes: Existing package metadata can be cited for inventory and gate state; body copy generation remains out of scope.

claim_trace_requirements: Inventory claims must cite specific package path and readiness flags.
Claims about counts must be revalidated by the current task's local checks before promotion.

evidence_refs.summary: The master catalog manifest reports 13 indexed packages, no new body copy, not-runtime status, ready_for_asset_review=true, and ready_for_pilot/runtime/production=false.
limitations: Inventory source only; counts and readiness must be rechecked by validator harness and gap audit.

claim_trace_policy.public_payload_requirement: Only claims with approved source rows and explicit public payload allowlist may enter candidate selector assets.
claim_trace_policy.share_payload_requirement: Shareable claims require share_safety_registry transformation and no raw score, vector, percentile, private id, or internal metadata exposure.

next_stage_blockers: No executable harness exists in this PR.
This ledger does not validate selector assets.
This ledger does not produce draft, repaired, or final assets.
